title: How Social Media Reshapes Beauty Standards
subtitle: 社交媒体如何重塑美的标准

paragraphs.en: An awakening has been taking place in the physical world against the beauty model that has been dictated to us for years. But in the digital arena, social media determines what is considered beautiful. The two opposing struggles are taking place in parallel. In the physical world, the struggle goes against the underlying pressure exerted on women to conform to an unrealistic beauty ideal. As part of the struggle, various media outlets have presented women whose bodies don't correspond to the so-called ideal.
paragraphs.zh: 现实世界正在发生一场觉醒，反对多年来强加给我们的美丽模式。但在数字领域，社交媒体决定了什么是美。两种对立的斗争正在同时进行。在现实世界中，这场斗争对抗的是强加给女性以符合不切实际美丽理想的潜在压力。作为斗争的一部分，各类媒体纷纷展示身体不符合所谓理想的女性。

paragraphs.en: All those women who had previously been excluded from the covers of magazines and television series have become 'legitimate'. At the same time, a group of influencers have begun to upload photos of themselves without makeup, and even photos in which they highlight supposed flaws. Technology has reshaped our beauty ideal and is doing a great job communicating that message to the masses. One of the bizarre legacies of the past decade is the popularity of the 'cyborg look', which illustrates what Americans will look like in 2050.
paragraphs.zh: 那些以前被排除在杂志封面和电视剧之外的女性，如今变得“正当”了。与此同时，一群网红开始上传自己未化妆的照片，甚至强调所谓缺陷的照片。技术重塑了我们的美丽理想，并在向大众传达这一信息方面做得非常出色。过去十年的奇异遗产之一是“赛博格造型”的流行，它描绘了2050年美国人的样子。

paragraphs.en: The cyborg look spread rapidly. Today, however, the Instagram face has become the new beauty ideal. The internalization of accepted beauty norms is much more effective when there is active involvement in the learning process. The active involvement of users is reflected in the gamified interaction offered by social media platforms — the ability to like, comment, compare, share. Once the desired beauty ideal has been internalized, users are given tools to change their appearance to suit the accepted ideal, such as editing the image, choosing the ideal filter, or the right background.
paragraphs.zh: 赛博格造型迅速传播。然而如今，Instagram面孔已成为新的美丽理想。当学习过程有主动参与时，公认美丽规范的内化会更加有效。用户的主动参与体现在社交媒体平台提供的游戏化互动中——点赞、评论、比较、分享的能力。一旦内化了理想的美丽标准，用户就会获得工具来改变外貌以适应公认理想，比如编辑图像、选择理想滤镜或合适背景。

paragraphs.en: A survey conducted in the United States revealed that more than 50 percent of people filter their images before posting them, and the majority of them are women. One of the significant consequences of obsessive filtering is the emerging tendency to treat oneself as an object to be observed and valued, in the same way another person observes and judges from the side. The effect of the filters is already far beyond amiable amusement. Apps like Instagram, Snapchat and FaceTune allow users to achieve a level of perfection previously only observed in beauty magazines.
paragraphs.zh: 美国一项调查显示，超过50%的人在发布图片前会使用滤镜，其中大多数是女性。强迫性过滤的重要后果之一是，人们开始倾向于将自己视为被观察和评价的对象，就像他人从旁观察和评判一样。滤镜的影响早已超出友善的娱乐范畴。Instagram、Snapchat和FaceTune等应用让用户能达到以前只有美容杂志中才能见到的完美程度。

words.meaning: adj. 和蔼可亲的，友善的

paragraphs.en: Even though humanity has always cherished beauty, in the last decade our obsession with looks has reached an unprecedented peak. The time spent on social media creates an urge to achieve an impossible beauty ideal so powerful that the only thing that can fix it is not cosmetic intervention, but mental health care.
paragraphs.zh: 尽管人类一向珍视美丽，但在过去十年里，我们对外表的痴迷达到了前所未有的顶峰。花在社交媒体上的时间催生出一种追求不可能实现的美丽理想的强烈冲动，以至于唯一能解决它的不是美容干预，而是心理健康护理。
